edges.description: Enzyme deficiency leads to lysosomal accumulation of undegraded glycopeptides.
Endothelial lysosomal storage explains the diffuse angiokeratoma phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: Glycopeptide storage in cutaneous vascular endothelium.

edges.description: Systemic glycopeptide storage plausibly contributes to progressive lymphatic dysfunction.
Multisystem storage disease involvement explains the coarse facial gestalt.
Lysosomal glycopeptide accumulation contributes to peripheral nervous system involvement.
Storage-related neurologic involvement explains recurrent sensorineural hearing impairment.
Storage-related cochleovestibular involvement explains recurrent vertigo attacks.
Lysosomal storage disease involvement explains mild cognitive impairment in the adult form.

nodes.description: Biallelic NAGA variants abolish lysosomal alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase, the hydrolase that removes terminal alpha-N-acetylgalactosamine residues from glycopeptides and glycolipids.

evidence.reference_title: Structural and immunocytochemical studies on alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency (Schindler/Kanzaki disease).
evidence.snippet: Alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (alpha-NAGA) deficiency (Schindler/Kanzaki disease) is a clinically and pathologically heterogeneous genetic disease with a wide spectrum including an early onset neuroaxonal dystrophy (Schindler disease) and late onset angiokeratoma corporis diffusum (Kanzaki disease)

nodes.description: Diffuse cutaneous angiokeratomas are the hallmark and often presenting feature of the adult Kanzaki form.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: Schindler disease type II (also known as Kanzaki disease) is characterised by lymphoedema, angiokeratoma corporis diffusum, neurological involvement, peripheral neuropathy, sensorineural hearing loss and recurrent vertigo attacks

nodes.description: Coarse facies reflecting systemic glycoconjugate storage.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: the patient had a coarse facies and bilateral symmetrical massive lymphoedema

nodes.description: Chronic, often massive and slowly progressive lymphedema.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: the patient had a coarse facies and bilateral symmetrical massive lymphoedema

nodes.description: Failure to degrade terminal alpha-N-acetylgalactosamine causes lysosomal accumulation of Tn-antigen (GalNAc-alpha-1-O-Ser/Thr)-bearing O-linked glycopeptides in fibroblasts and vascular endothelium, with urinary excretion of sialylated glycoconjugates.

evidence.reference_title: Structural and immunocytochemical studies on alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency (Schindler/Kanzaki disease).
evidence.snippet: the main lysosomal accumulated material in cultured fibroblasts from patients with Kanzaki disease is Tn-antigen

nodes.description: Mild intellectual impairment distinguishes the adult form from the severe infantile type.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: an adult-onset disorder with mild intellectual disability

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: an apparently homozygous c.577G>T (p.Glu193*) mutation in the NAGA gene

nodes.description: Axonal and demyelinating polyneuropathy, often with neuropathic pain.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: a 68-year-old man presenting with axonal and demyelinating polyneuropathy

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing impairment is a recurrent neurological feature.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: neurological involvement, peripheral neuropathy, sensorineural hearing loss and recurrent vertigo attacks

nodes.description: Recurrent vertigo attacks are reported as part of the neurological involvement.

evidence.reference_title: A New Case of Schindler Disease.
evidence.snippet: sensorineural hearing loss and recurrent vertigo attacks
